headline: Debt stays at 93% of GDP as Ofgem cap falls and Lords blocks pension investment mandation

items.title: Ofgem cuts April to June energy price cap to £1,641
items.summary: Ofgem set the typical dual-fuel Direct Debit cap at £1,641 for 1 Apr-30 Jun 2026, down 6.6% from £1,758 in the previous quarter.

items.title: ONS: UK borrowed £14.3bn in Feb 2026; debt 93.1% of GDP
items.summary: ONS reported borrowing of £14.3bn in Feb 2026, £2.2bn higher than Feb 2025 and £6.9bn above the OBR forecast; net debt was 93.1% of GDP.

items.title: Lords vote to remove ministers’ pension investment mandation power
items.summary: House of Lords backed an amendment (217-113) to remove the government’s reserve power to direct DC pension investments tied to Mansion House Accord commitments.

items.title: GOV.UK experimental indicator: 191,300 net additional homes delivered since 1 Apr 2025
items.summary: Experimental housing supply indicators estimate 191,300 net additional homes delivered in England between 1 Apr 2025 and 15 Mar 2026, and 342,100 since 9 Jul 2024.
